text.title: ai-governance-compliance Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ai-governance-compliance as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Documentation written after deployment, not before — the model card describes what was shipped instead of being a deployment gate.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: EU AI Act Article 11 + 16 require the technical file before placement on the market; post-hoc docs are not legally compliant.

failure-mode.description: Bias evaluation against benchmark dataset (e.g. UCI Adult) instead of production distribution.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Benchmark fairness does not predict production fairness; a model that passes on benchmark may exhibit adverse impact on real users.

failure-mode.description: Agent self-approves its own deployment via a config flag.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Article 14 (human oversight) requires independent review for high-risk AI; self-approval voids the legal compliance claim.

failure-mode.description: Audit log writes via streaming append without flush+fsync.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Crash mid-write produces a truncated final line; auditors reject corrupt logs and the deployment loses traceability.

failure-mode.description: SHAP explanations cached and re-served instead of computed per-prediction for high-risk decisions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each prediction has a unique attribution profile; serving stale explanations misleads regulators and end users.
